data.title: TELITE LMS — System Architecture Board
data.html:
TELITE LMS — System Architecture Board

Current state · Environment flow · Database connections · Confusion points
● Current Active Setup
⚡
FastAPI Backend
Running locally via uvicorn on :8001
Config source: telite-backend/.env
DB backend: TELITE_DB_BACKEND=sqlite
🗄
SQLite Database
File: telite-backend/data/telite_lms.db
No Postgres credentials needed
Simplest local-dev mode
🖥
React Frontend
Dev server on :3000 or :5173
Calls backend at localhost:8001
◆ Docker Compose Stack
🐘
PostgreSQL Container
postgres:14 · Port 55432→5432
Bootstrap: POSTGRES_DB=moodle
POSTGRES_USER=moodleuser
Volume: telite_pgdata
🎓
Moodle PHP Application
Port 8082→80
Config: docker-config.php
Uses MOODLE_DB_* env vars
DB: moodle via moodleuser
🔧
Backend Container
Port 8001
⚠ Uses ${POSTGRES_*} for backend creds
TELITE_POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DB}
TELITE_POSTGRES_USER=${POSTGRES_USER}
🌐
Frontend Container
Port 3000→80 · Nginx
📁 Environment Variable Sources
Root .env
POSTGRES_DBmoodle
POSTGRES_USERmoodleuser
POSTGRES_PASSWORDCHANGE_ME…
TELITE_DB_BACKENDpostgres
TELITE_DATABASE_URL…postgres:postgres123…
TELITE_POSTGRES_DBtelite_backend_db
TELITE_POSTGRES_USERpostgres
MOODLE_DB_NAMEmoodle
MOODLE_DB_USERmoodleuser
telite-backend/.env
TELITE_DB_BACKENDsqlite
TELITE_DATABASE_URL(empty)
TELITE_POSTGRES_HOSTlocalhost
TELITE_POSTGRES_PORT55432
TELITE_POSTGRES_DBtelite_backend_db
TELITE_POSTGRES_USERpostgres
TELITE_POSTGRES_PASSWORDpostgres123
docker-config.php
dbtypepgsql
dbhostMOODLE_DB_HOST
dbnameMOODLE_DB_NAME
dbuserMOODLE_DB_USER
dbpassMOODLE_DB_PASSWORD
prefixmdl_
🔗 Database Connection Paths
ACTIVE NOW — Local dev
FastAPI (uvicorn) → telite-backend/.env → SQLite file DB ✓
DOCKER — Moodle path
Moodle PHP → docker-config.php → MOODLE_DB_* → PostgreSQL:moodle ✓
DOCKER — Backend path ⚠ PROBLEM
Backend container → compose env → TELITE_POSTGRES_DB=${POSTGRES_DB}=moodle → Same DB as Moodle! ✗
store.py DSN fallback chain
① TELITE_DATABASE_URL → ② TELITE_POSTGRES_* → ③ MOODLE_DB_* → ④ POSTGRES_*
⚠ Current Confusion Points
1
Docker compose shares one credential path. TELITE_POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB} means the backend container gets moodle as its database name — the same DB Moodle uses. Backend tables and mdl_* tables coexist.
2
Root .env has conflicting credential sets. POSTGRES_USER=moodleuser but TELITE_POSTGRES_USER=postgres. The compose file ignores TELITE_* and uses POSTGRES_* for the backend.
3
No init script for second DB/user. PostgreSQL container only creates the database from POSTGRES_DB on first init. There is no /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/ script to create telite_backend DB or telite_backend_user.
4
Backend .env previously tried separate Postgres creds (postgres:postgres123@localhost:55432/telite_backend_db) but the server rejected them — that DB/user didn't exist. Now reverted to SQLite.
✓ Backend currently uses SQLite
The local backend runs independently on SQLite. No Postgres dependency for local dev. This is the simplest working mode right now.
ℹ Backend code already supports separation
store.py has a full DSN fallback chain: TELITE_DATABASE_URL → TELITE_POSTGRES_* → MOODLE_DB_* → POSTGRES_*. No code changes needed.
⚠ Moodle still expects PostgreSQL
docker-config.php reads MOODLE_DB_* vars. Moodle is always Postgres-backed. It cannot use SQLite.
⚠ Docker hybrid not implemented yet
The remaining work is Docker/env cleanup: init script, compose rewiring, and proper credential separation. Not app logic.

data.title: TELITE LMS — Setup Comparison & Evolution
data.html:
TELITE LMS — Setup Comparison & Evolution

Current mixed → Temporary SQLite → Final hybrid PostgreSQL
Current Mixed
→
Temporary SQLite
→
Final Hybrid PG
🔴 1. Current Mixed Setup
Backend Runtime
FastAPI via uvicorn
Switches between SQLite & Postgres depending on env
Database Used
Mixed
Root .env → postgres
Backend .env → sqlite (overrides)
Docker Usage
Compose available but backend wired to ${POSTGRES_*}
Shares Moodle creds
Moodle Config
docker-config.php → MOODLE_DB_*
Connects to moodle DB
Key Env Vars
POSTGRES_DB=moodle
POSTGRES_USER=moodleuser
TELITE_POSTGRES_DB=telite_backend_db
Compose ignores TELITE_* for backend
Advantages
✓ Works today (backend on SQLite)
✓ Moodle config is correct
Problems
✗ Docker backend → same DB as Moodle
✗ Conflicting credential sets in root .env
✗ No second DB or user exists in Postgres
✗ Previous Postgres attempt failed
🟡 2. Temporary SQLite Local-Dev
Backend Runtime
FastAPI via uvicorn --reload
Port 8001
Database Used
SQLite only
TELITE_DB_BACKEND=sqlite
File: data/telite_lms.db
Docker Usage
Not used for backend
Moodle containers may still run separately
Moodle Config
Still uses MOODLE_DB_* → Postgres
Moodle works independently
Key Env Vars
TELITE_DB_BACKEND=sqlite
TELITE_DATABASE_URL= (empty)
No Postgres vars needed
Advantages
✓ Zero Postgres dependency
✓ Fastest local startup
✓ No credential confusion
✓ No Docker needed for backend
Problems
✗ Not production-representative
✗ SQLite ≠ Postgres behavior
✗ No full-stack Docker test
✗ Data not shared with Moodle
🟢 3. Final Hybrid PostgreSQL
Backend Runtime
FastAPI in Docker container
Port 8001
Database Used
Two separate DBs
moodle → Moodle only
telite_backend → Backend only
Docker Usage
Full stack via docker-compose
Init script in /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/
Creates both DBs & users
Moodle Config
MOODLE_DB_* → moodle DB
moodleuser — isolated access
Key Env Vars
POSTGRES_USER=postgres (admin)
MOODLE_DB_NAME=moodle
MOODLE_DB_USER=moodleuser
TELITE_POSTGRES_DB=telite_backend
TELITE_POSTGRES_USER=telite_backend_user
Clean separation
Advantages
✓ Full DB isolation
✓ Production-ready architecture
✓ Separate users & permissions
✓ One Postgres server (efficient)
✓ Clean env variable model
Problems
✗ Requires init script creation
✗ Existing volumes need reinit
✗ Compose rewiring needed
🔄 Dependency Flow — How the System Evolves
1
Current Mixed
Backend → telite-backend/.env → SQLite file
Backend (Docker) → ${POSTGRES_*} → moodle DB ✗
Moodle → MOODLE_DB_* → moodle DB ✓
Switch backend .env to SQLite · Decouple from Postgres
2
Temporary SQLite
Backend → TELITE_DB_BACKEND=sqlite → SQLite ✓
Moodle → MOODLE_DB_* → moodle DB ✓
Docker stack → Not refactored yet
Add init script · Rewire compose · Create second DB/user
3
Final Hybrid PostgreSQL
Backend → TELITE_POSTGRES_* → telite_backend DB ✓
Moodle → MOODLE_DB_* → moodle DB ✓
Postgres container → init.sql → 2 DBs + 2 users ✓
Bottom line: The backend code is already capable of full separation. The remaining work is infrastructure: (1) Create a Postgres init SQL script that bootstraps both moodle and telite_backend databases with separate users. (2) Rewire docker-compose.yml so the backend container reads TELITE_POSTGRES_* instead of ${POSTGRES_*}. (3) Update .env.example and docs to reflect the clean two-database model. SQLite stays as the simplest local-dev fallback — no need to remove it.

data.markdown:
## Architecture Workspace Navigation Guide

The canvas is structured into three vertical tiers across two main columns:

### 1. Top Section (y: 20px)
- **← Left Column**: **System Architecture Board** — Shows active local setup (SQLite) and current Docker Compose stack with identified bug points.
- **→ Right Column**: **Setup Comparison & Evolution** — A 3-column comparative view of Current Mixed vs Temporary SQLite vs Final Hybrid Postgres configurations, including transition maps.

### 2. Middle Section (y: 1180px)
- **← Left Column**: **Docker Compose Wiring — Before & After** — Illustrates exactly where `docker-compose.yml` env vars cross-contaminate and shows the SQL setup script layout.
- **→ Right Column**: **Navigation Guide** (This Card).

### 3. Bottom Section (y: 1900px)
- **← Left Column**: **Production-Grade Architecture** — Detailed network, security, and container diagram for the target architecture (one Postgres server, logical boundaries, isolated user access).
- **→ Right Column**: **Env Variable Relationship Map** — Interactive visualization mapping `.env` and `telite-backend/.env` sources through Docker and PHP to the `store.py` resolution priority path (1st to 4th chain).
data.title: 🗺 TELITE LMS — Navigation Guide
Docker Compose Wiring — Before & After
data.html:
Docker Compose Wiring — Before & After

Showing the exact YAML changes and init script needed for the hybrid split
✗ Current docker-compose.yml (backend section)
backend:
  build:
    context: ./telite-backend
  environment:
    TELITE_DB_BACKEND: ${TELITE_DB_BACKEND:-postgres}
    TELITE_POSTGRES_HOST: postgres
    TELITE_POSTGRES_PORT: 5432
    TELITE_POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB}          # ← resolves to "moodle"
    TELITE_POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER}      # ← resolves to "moodleuser"
    TELITE_POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} # ← Moodle's password
✓ Fixed docker-compose.yml (backend section)
backend:
  build:
    context: ./telite-backend
  environment:
    TELITE_DB_BACKEND: postgres
    TELITE_POSTGRES_HOST: postgres
    TELITE_POSTGRES_PORT: 5432
    TELITE_POSTGRES_DB: ${TELITE_POSTGRES_DB}          # → "telite_backend"
    TELITE_POSTGRES_USER: ${TELITE_POSTGRES_USER}      # → "telite_backend_user"
    TELITE_POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${TELITE_POSTGRES_PASSWORD} # → separate password
The bug: Lines 55–57 in current compose use ${POSTGRES_DB}, ${POSTGRES_USER}, ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} for the backend service. These are Moodle's bootstrap credentials. The backend ends up connecting to the moodle database with moodleuser.
The fix: Change to ${TELITE_POSTGRES_DB}, ${TELITE_POSTGRES_USER}, ${TELITE_POSTGRES_PASSWORD}. These resolve to the backend's own credentials, ensuring complete DB isolation.
📄 docker/init/01-create-databases.sql (NEW)
-- This script runs on first Postgres container initialization only.
-- Mount into /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/ in docker-compose.yml

-- 1. Moodle database & user
CREATE DATABASE moodle;
CREATE USER moodleuser WITH PASSWORD 'moodlepass';
GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE moodle TO moodleuser;

-- 2. Backend database & user
CREATE DATABASE telite_backend;
CREATE USER telite_backend_user WITH PASSWORD 'change_me_backend_pass';
GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON DATABASE telite_backend TO telite_backend_user;

-- 3. Revoke cross-access
REVOKE ALL ON DATABASE moodle FROM telite_backend_user;
REVOKE ALL ON DATABASE telite_backend FROM moodleuser;

Final .env Variable Layout

Root .env — clean two-database credential model
VariableCurrent ValueFinal ValueUsed By
▸ PostgreSQL Container Bootstrap
POSTGRES_USER moodleuser postgres Container init only (superuser)
POSTGRES_PASSWORD CHANGE_ME… strong_admin_pass Container init only
POSTGRES_DB moodle postgres Default DB (not used by apps)
▸ Moodle Application
MOODLE_DB_HOST moodle_db_final postgres (container name) Moodle PHP
MOODLE_DB_NAMEmoodlemoodle ✓Moodle PHP
MOODLE_DB_USERmoodleusermoodleuser ✓Moodle PHP
MOODLE_DB_PASSWORDmoodlepassmoodlepass ✓Moodle PHP
▸ Backend Application
TELITE_POSTGRES_HOST localhost postgres (Docker) / localhost (local) Backend only
TELITE_POSTGRES_DBtelite_backend_dbtelite_backendBackend only
TELITE_POSTGRES_USERpostgrestelite_backend_userBackend only
TELITE_POSTGRES_PASSWORDpostgres123change_me_backend_passBackend only
TELITE_DB_BACKEND sqlite (local) / postgres (Docker) postgres (always in Docker) Backend only

data.title: TELITE LMS — Production Architecture Diagram
data.html:
TELITE LMS — Production-Grade Architecture Diagram

Single PostgreSQL Server · Logically Isolated Databases · Strict Credential & Network Boundaries
🔍 Component Legend
Reverse Proxy (Nginx)
Moodle Container (PHP)
Backend Container (Python)
Postgres Server (Port 5432)
SQLite Local Fallback
Strict Isolation Boundary
⚙ Architecture Rules
1. Strict DB Isolation: Users cannot access or query tables in the other database.

2. Decoupled Environments: Moodle never reads TELITE_*, and the FastAPI backend never depends on MOODLE_*.

3. One Server: Low resource usage by avoiding a second heavy PostgreSQL instance.
🛡 Authentication Flow
- Frontend Client obtains tokens directly from the FastAPI Auth Session layer.

- FastAPI Backend calls the Moodle API using MOODLE_TOKEN via isolated internal service host.
🌐 Web Client / Browser
(Requests over HTTPS)
🟢 telite_proxy
Nginx Reverse Proxy
Routes /moodle/ → telite_moodle:80
Routes /api/ → telite_backend:8001
Routes / → telite_frontend:80
Reverse Proxy Routing ▼
🟠 telite_moodle Moodle
Apache + PHP 8.1 Runtime
Config: docker-config.php
DB Access: moodleuser only
Variables: MOODLE_DB_*
⚡ REST Sync API
Backend syncs organizations/users
🔵 telite_backend FastAPI
FastAPI + Uvicorn Runtime
Config: store.py DSN resolver
DB Access: telite_backend_user only
Variables: TELITE_POSTGRES_*
▼
🟣 telite_db PostgreSQL 14 Container
Container Instance: Port 5432 internally (mapped to 55432 host)
Volume mapping: telite_pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
Database: moodle
Owner: moodleuser
Contains: Moodle Core + plugin tables (mdl_*)
Isolation: Access restricted to moodle user.
Database: telite_backend
Owner: telite_backend_user
Contains: FastAPI Platform tables (users, category scope, audit, logs)
Isolation: Access restricted to backend user.
🛡 SECURITY BOUNDARY
Strict DB user permission boundaries are applied via Postgres grants. The user moodleuser cannot execute queries or see tables inside telite_backend database. Conversely, telite_backend_user cannot access Moodle's moodle database tables.
⚙ Local-Dev fallback SQLite Mode
When running local development outside Docker, the FastAPI backend falls back to SQLite:
- TELITE_DB_BACKEND=sqlite
- Local Database File: telite-backend/data/telite_lms.db
- Zero credentials required for database operations.
🐳 Orchestration Init Details
A Postgres setup script at docker/init/01-create-databases.sql is mounted inside the container's /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/. On container first boot, it creates both databases and credentials automatically.

data.title: TELITE LMS — Env Variable Relationship Map
data.html:
TELITE LMS — Environment Variable Relationship Map

Visualizing variable sources, mappings, conflict states, and backend fallback priority chains
📥 Variable Sources & Docker Mappings
1. Master Config: root .env Source File
MOODLE_DB_NAMEmoodle
MOODLE_DB_USERmoodleuser
TELITE_POSTGRES_DBtelite_backend
TELITE_POSTGRES_USERtelite_backend_user
POSTGRES_DBpostgres (Bootstrap user)
2. Local Dev: telite-backend/.env SQLite Context
Used during uvicorn app.main:app runs. Under SQLite mode, it decouples the local server from Postgres:
TELITE_DB_BACKENDsqlite
TELITE_DATABASE_URL(empty)
TELITE_SQLITE_SOURCE_PATHtelite_lms.db
▼ Environment Mappings ▼
3. Orchestration Map: docker-compose.yml Mapping Layer
moodle.environment.MOODLE_DB_NAME${MOODLE_DB_NAME}
backend.environment.TELITE_POSTGRES_DB${TELITE_POSTGRES_DB}
4. Moodle Config: docker-config.php PHP Mapping
$CFG->dbnamegetenv('MOODLE_DB_NAME')
$CFG->dbusergetenv('MOODLE_DB_USER')
🛡 Backend Resolution Priority (store.py)
The FastAPI store module resolved DSN using the following priority order:
1
TELITE_DATABASE_URL
Direct Postgres URI specification string. If present, it bypasses all other variables.
2
TELITE_POSTGRES_* Group
Separate backend connection credentials (TELITE_POSTGRES_DB, TELITE_POSTGRES_USER).
3
MOODLE_DB_* Fallback (Legacy)
Checks for Moodle DB variables. Left for compatibility in simple single-db setups.
4
POSTGRES_* Fallback (Legacy)
Last-resort DB variables. Resolves to standard Postgres settings.
🚨 Identified Conflicts & Issues
Duplicate Credential Mappings in Compose
The backend container was mapping TELITE_POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB}. Because POSTGRES_DB=moodle, the backend was forced to use the Moodle database. The separate TELITE_POSTGRES_DB variable in the root `.env` was ignored.
SQLite Fallback Behavior
If TELITE_DB_BACKEND is set to sqlite, the resolution engine bypasses the entire PostgreSQL fallback priority chain and connects to the local SQLite database path. This prevents local runs from crashing when no Postgres server is running.
